name: blood_bank_expiring_units_research_immunology
premise: Blood center medical director Alex Kim must decide whether to divert forty-eight near-expiry platelet units to an immunology study that could improve alloimmunization protocols nationwide. Transfusion chief Ines Bennet argues rural hospitals already booked those units for oncology patients arriving tonight. The study protocol allows only fresh-draw windows; delaying research voids a federal grant that funds cold-chain upgrades. Redirecting units may strand documented patients while advancing science that helps thousands later. Hospital partners expect daily allocation forecasts, and any misstep could force cancellation of surgeries that cannot be rescheduled quickly. External scrutiny from regulators and community observers has intensified because the outcome could redefine expectations in Healthcare.

agents.name: Alex Kim
agents.identity: I am the blood center medical director balancing inventory stewardship, research commitments signed by the board, and equitable distribution contracts with rural hospitals. I know that whichever policy I choose will be scrutinized after the fact as if the risk had been obvious.
agents.goal: I want research that saves future patients without breaking trust with hospitals that scheduled platelets around these lots. I need a choice that is defensible if the worst case happens, not just the most convenient today.

agents.name: Ines Bennet
agents.identity: I am the transfusion service chief who matches platelets to refractory oncology patients with antibody complexity and narrow compatibility. I know that whichever policy I choose will be scrutinized after the fact as if the risk had been obvious.
agents.goal: I want every booked unit to reach a named patient tonight rather than expiring in a lab arm that could reschedule with grant flexibility. I need a choice that is defensible if the worst case happens, not just the most convenient today.

choices: I will divert the near-expiry units to the immunology study under the grant window and pay for emergency courier platelets from a partner region, accepting six-hour delays if couriers miss connections.
I will honor all rural hospital bookings and cancel the study draw for this cycle, accepting grant clawback risk and delayed cold-chain upgrades that affect next summer's inventory.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The research coordinator and oncology charge nurse are both on hold for opposite orders. Alex Kim must choose between diverting units to research or honoring rural bookings. What does Alex Kim do?
